statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: Archived audit trail only. Keep this out of the normal tweak surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Service host grouping in Windows 10
links.summary: Microsoft documents svchost service refactoring, explains that service splitting depends on memory thresholds, and describes the general service-splitting behavior on modern Windows.

links.title: Repo system research notes for service splitting
links.summary: The repo documents the SvcHostSplitThresholdInKB path, describes the threshold behavior, and lists 0xFFFFFFFF as the applied no-splitting override.

links.title: Decompiled SCM configuration reader for service splitting
links.summary: The decompiled service-control-manager helper calls RegQueryValueExW for SvcHostSplitThresholdInKB under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control, showing the threshold is consumed by the svchost grouping logic.

links.title: Current app implementation
links.summary: The provider writes SvcHostSplitThresholdInKB = -1 (0xFFFFFFFF) for the current tweak.
